varieties.name: Snowball
varieties.traits: Heirloom, open-pollinated; Compact plants with tight, dense snow-white heads 15cm diameter. Medium-sized curds weighing 500g-750g.
varieties.yield_notes: 9-12 weeks to maturity. Good productivity for home gardens.

varieties.name: Sixty Days
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated; Pure white, firm, solid heads. Compact growth suitable for warmer areas.
varieties.yield_notes: Early harvest - ready in just 60 days from transplant. High productivity.
varieties.availability: Happy Valley Seeds, ABSeeds humid subtropical climates, Love of Dirt (Queensland supplier)

varieties.name: Selection 174 (Quickheart)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated; Creamy white, tightly packed heads 15-20cm diameter. Heavy, medium-sized heads. Strong inner leaves provide excellent self-wrapping protection.
varieties.yield_notes: 13-15 weeks to maturity. Reliable, consistent producer.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection Australia, Dave's Seed, Australian Seed, Mr Fothergill's Australia, Rangeview Seeds, New Seeds

varieties.name: First Early
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Fast-growing small cauliflower with compact plants. Firm small heads to 500g.

varieties.name: All Year Round
varieties.traits: Heirloom, open-pollinated; Medium-sized firm heads with dense curds. Large green protective leaves.

varieties.name: Purple of Sicily
varieties.traits: Heirloom, open-pollinated; Brilliant purple heads, 2-3 pounds (1-1.5kg). Stunning deep purple florets with creamy base.
varieties.yield_notes: 75-90 days to maturity. Vigorous, productive plants.

varieties.name: Romanesco (Romanesco Broccoli)
varieties.traits: Heirloom, open-pollinated (some modern F1 hybrids also available); Chartreuse/lime green color. Striking fractal spiral pattern with conical pointed florets. Heads 5-6 inches (12-15cm) wide, can weigh up to 5 pounds. Mathematical marvel based on Fibonacci sequence.
varieties.yield_notes: 75-100 days to maturity. Compact, tender heads with firm texture.
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, Baker Creek Seeds (ships internationally), Botanical Interests, multiple Australian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Cheddar (Orange Cauliflower)
varieties.origin: Canada, 1970 (natural mutation), developed at Cornell University USA
varieties.traits: Hybrid (F1); Bright orange to cheddar-orange colored heads. Medium to large dome-shaped curds. Color deepens when cooked. Contains 25% more vitamin A than white cauliflower.
varieties.yield_notes: 70-80 days to maturity. Good field-holding ability.

varieties.name: Green Macerata
varieties.traits: Heirloom, open-pollinated; Vibrant lime-green heads. Compact, tender texture with fine flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: 13-16 weeks to harvest. Vigorous, early-bearing variety.

varieties.name: Alverda (Green Cauliflower)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated green broccoflower type; Large lime-green heads up to 1.5 pounds. Greenish-purple leaves. Deep green coloring.

varieties.name: Early Kunwari 🌏
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated Indian variety, INDIGENOUS to India; Early variety suitable for Indian conditions. Named for September-October maturity period (Kunwar month).

varieties.name: Early Patna 🏆
varieties.origin: India, 1929 (listed by Sutton and Sons India), Patna region
varieties.traits: Heirloom, INDIGENOUS Indian variety - one of first four Indian varieties; Early maturing tropical type. Open habit with exposed yellow-cream curds. Long stalk, characteristic of Indian types.
varieties.yield_notes: Early maturity, September-October harvest when sown May-July.

varieties.name: Early Benaras 🏆
varieties.origin: India, 1929 (listed by Sutton and Sons India), Benaras/Varanasi region
varieties.traits: Heirloom, INDIGENOUS Indian variety - one of first four Indian varieties; Similar characteristics to Early Patna. Early tropical type with open growth habit.

varieties.name: Pusa Katki 🌏
varieties.origin: India, IARI (Indian Agricultural Research Institute) New Delhi
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, INDIGENOUS Indian variety; Early variety named for October-November maturity (Kartik month). Medium-sized solid white curds.
varieties.availability: IARI seed sales, Indian government agriculture departments, tropical monsoon climates agricultural universities, local seed suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Deepali 🌏
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, INDIGENOUS Indian variety; Early maturing variety developed for Northern India. Compact, slightly conical creamish-white curds. Self-blanching habit.
varieties.availability: IARI seed distribution, Indian agricultural departments, tropical monsoon climates seed outlets

varieties.name: Pusa Early Synthetic 🌏
varieties.traits: Synthetic variety (multiple inbreds crossed), INDIGENOUS; Main season variety with compact creamy-white to white curds.
varieties.availability: IARI sources, government agriculture offices, seed distribution centers

varieties.name: Kimaya
varieties.traits: Tropical hybrid (F1); Vigorous plant with dark green semi-erect leaves. Good self-blanching. Dome-shaped curds. Curd weight 1-1.25 kg.
varieties.yield_notes: 50-55 days after planting. Moderate heat tolerance.
varieties.availability: Syngenta India distributors, tropical monsoon climates agricultural supply stores, regional seed dealers throughout tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Barsati
varieties.traits: Tropical hybrid (F1); Semi-erect vigorous plant with light blue-green leaves. Dome-shaped compact white curd. Average weight 0.5-0.8 kg.
varieties.yield_notes: 45-50 days after planting - extremely fast. Heat-tolerant.
varieties.availability: Syngenta India, widespread availability in tropical monsoon climates seed shops, agricultural supply centers

varieties.name: Pawas
varieties.traits: Tropical hybrid (F1); Tall, erect plant with vigorous growth. Green, broad leaves. Compact dome-shaped creamish-white curd. Weight 1-1.25 kg.
varieties.yield_notes: 50-55 days after planting. Moderate heat tolerance.

varieties.name: Summer King
varieties.traits: Hybrid (F1); Whitish round-shaped curd. Average weight 400-500g.
varieties.availability: Indian seed companies, tropical monsoon climates agricultural supply centers

varieties.name: Swarna
varieties.traits: Hybrid; White, compact curds. Average weight 1-2.5 kg.

varieties.name: Pant Gobhi-2 🌏
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, INDIGENOUS; Early variety developed for northern India.
varieties.availability: Pantnagar university, Indian government seed programs

varieties.name: Pant Gobhi-3 🌏
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, INDIGENOUS; Similar to Pant Gobhi-2, early variety.

varieties.name: Pant Shubhra 🌏
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated synthetic variety, INDIGENOUS; Main season variety. Compact, slightly conical, creamish-white, non-ricey, non-leafy curds.
varieties.availability: Pantnagar university seed sales, Indian agricultural departments, tropical monsoon climates seed centers

varieties.name: Purple of Sicily
varieties.traits: Heirloom, open-pollinated; (See humid subtropical climates section for full details) Brilliant purple heads 2-3 pounds.

varieties.name: Romanesco
varieties.traits: Heirloom; (See humid subtropical climates section for full details) Chartreuse fractal spiral pattern.
varieties.availability: International suppliers, limited success expected in tropical monsoon climates climate
